items.briefing:
Scammers are continuing to target Australians, so staff should stay alert to suspicious messages or requests. Separately, a new threat has been identified where ChatGPT shared links are being used to host fake "outage" pages that trick users into downloading malware — if you click a ChatGPT link and see an unexpected error or outage message asking you to install something, do not proceed.

A recent report also highlights that AI chatbots can use subtle design tricks to nudge users toward certain behaviours, so be cautious about over-trusting AI tools for sensitive work decisions.

**Tip:** If a website or AI tool unexpectedly asks you to download software, stop and check with your IT team first.

featured.title: ChatGPT share links abused to host fake outage pages to deliver malware - BleepingComputer
featured.quote: ChatGPT's sharing feature exploited to distribute malware via fake outage pages.
